86c72e35-8744-4f85-8620-345f11b8b8a1.label: text_chunk
86c72e35-8744-4f85-8620-345f11b8b8a1.text:
CONFIDENTIALITY AND NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This  Confidentiality  and  Non-Disclosure  Agreement  (“Agreement”)  is entered  into  by  and  between  ______________________________________ (“VENDOR”) and Arkansas State University (“UNIVERSITY”).

8f52ddbd-0882-4ecf-a6a8-4f7c9d8aa908.label: text_chunk
8f52ddbd-0882-4ecf-a6a8-4f7c9d8aa908.text:
WHEREAS,  the  parties  wish  to  confirm  their  understandings  with  respect to  the  confidential  nature  of  the  relationship  between  the  parties  relating  to  the transmission, use, and protection of certain confidential data, it is hereby agreed as follows:
1. Confidential Information.

40a0bc7e-0b30-4185-be89-714101d9f155.label: text_chunk
40a0bc7e-0b30-4185-be89-714101d9f155.text: VENDOR acknowledges that in the course of providing services to UNIVERSITY, VENDOR will be given or have access to confidential  student  and/or  employee  information  (“Confidential  Information”) protected  from  disclosure  by  privacy  laws.

5242abe8-64be-43a7-8a56-20a9632c94ba.label: text_chunk
5242abe8-64be-43a7-8a56-20a9632c94ba.text:
VENDOR,  its  employees,  agents, contractors  and  subcontractors  shall,  in  addition  to  their  respective  obligations hereunder,  use  best  efforts  at  all  times  to  adopt  and  follow  procedures  and practices to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and prevent its disclosure to others without the express written consent of UNIVERSITY.

VENDOR  specifically,

e9311983-e10b-4b15-b5e4-917ef80e4b03.label: text_chunk
e9311983-e10b-4b15-b5e4-917ef80e4b03.text: acknowledges  Federal  privacy  laws  such  as Gramm-Leach-Bliley  Act  (Title  15,  United  States  Code,  Sections  6801(b)  and 6805(b)(2))  applicable  to  financial  transactions  and  Family  Educational  Rights and  Privacy  Act  (Title  20,  United  States  Code,

8c447f80-ad1f-4d46-b5c3-f7ae4474165f.label: text_chunk
8c447f80-ad1f-4d46-b5c3-f7ae4474165f.text:
Section  1232g)  applicable  to student records and information from student records.

VENDOR  further  acknowledges  that  UNIVERSITY’S  willingness  to disclose Confidential Information to VENDOR is predicated on VENDOR entering into committing to abide by the terms of this Agreement and all applicable privacy laws.2. Nondisclosure.

2d80992e-8f02-469a-a55c-bc089307ee2b.label: text_chunk
2d80992e-8f02-469a-a55c-bc089307ee2b.text:
VENDOR,  its  employees,  agents,  contractors,  and subcontractors  shall  use  the  Confidential  Information  solely  in  connection  with performance  by  VENDOR  of  the  services  provided  to  UNIVERSITY  and  for  no other purpose.

  Neither VENDOR nor any of its employees, agents, contractors, or subcontractors shall sell, release, transfer, reprint, duplicate, recreate,

5ea72a00-bc0c-4670-beb0-02351009ea44.label: text_chunk
5ea72a00-bc0c-4670-beb0-02351009ea44.text:
disclose or permit the disclosure to any other person or entity of any of the Confidential Information  or  of  any  files,  compilation,  study,  report,  or  analysis  or  data  base containing,  based  on,  or  derived  from  the  Confidential  Information  without  the express  written  consent  of  UNIVERSITY  and  in  full  compliance  with  applicable state and federal privacy laws.

1

629e4145-ee0b-415c-b97f-b6ca4deb7e7c.label: text_chunk
629e4145-ee0b-415c-b97f-b6ca4deb7e7c.text: VENDOR  shall  maintain  the  privacy  of  protected  personal information  and  shall  be  financially  responsible,  if  and  to  the  extent  that  any security  breach  relating  to  protected  personal  information  results  from  acts  or omissions of VENDOR, or its personnel, for any notifications to affected persons (after  prompt  consultation  with  UNIVERSITY),

372813d9-ca14-4a58-a8c1-134584379352.label: text_chunk
372813d9-ca14-4a58-a8c1-134584379352.text:
and  to  the  extent  requested  by UNIVERSITY, administratively responsible for such notification.

3. Disclosure  Sought  Under  Legal  Process.

e9ea11ca-474b-4486-9435-82ea6558b955.label: text_chunk
e9ea11ca-474b-4486-9435-82ea6558b955.text: VENDOR  shall immediately notify UNIVERSITY in writing of any subpoena, court order, or other legal  process  seeking  or  purporting  to  compel  disclosure  of  any  of  the Confidential  Information  and  shall  challenge,  oppose,  or  appeal  any  such subpoena,  order,  or  legal  process  to  the  extent  deemed  appropriate  by UNIVERSITY.

6f4bc65b-22eb-4f09-a7af-5993a8bf7ec0.label: text_chunk
6f4bc65b-22eb-4f09-a7af-5993a8bf7ec0.text: In  no  event  shall  VENDOR  voluntarily,  without  a  court  order, disclose  or  permit  the  disclosure  of  any  of  the  Confidential  Information  in response  to  legal  process  unless  and  until  VENDOR  has  given  the  required notice to UNIVERSITY and VENDOR has exhausted any and all legal remedies available to it to limit or prevent the disclosure.4.

a00abb8f-573e-4bae-a1c6-851e3c8d0e9a.label: text_chunk
a00abb8f-573e-4bae-a1c6-851e3c8d0e9a.text:
Return  of  Materials.    Upon  request  of  UNIVERSITY  at  any  time VENDOR  shall  return  all  or  such  part  of  the  Confidential  Information  as UNIVERSITY may designate to be returned.

  In addition, upon the completion of the  services  provided  by  VENDOR  to  UNIVERSITY,  VENDOR  shall  return  or destroy, as UNIVERSITY may instruct,

09d25fe7-24b5-42bc-8a7f-211c628071c7.label: text_chunk
09d25fe7-24b5-42bc-8a7f-211c628071c7.text:
all Confidential Information in VENDOR’S possession  or  control,  whether  in  printed,  electronic  or  any  other  format, including all duplicates and copies thereof of any files, compilation, study, report, analysis,  or  data  base  containing,  based  on  or  derived  from  the  Confidential Information.

5. Loss  of  Access.

b11ff6ac-0dce-40da-b317-95170dc3f127.label: text_chunk
b11ff6ac-0dce-40da-b317-95170dc3f127.text:
VENDOR’S  disclosure  of  Confidential  Information to  third  parties  without  UNIVERSITY’S  authorization,  or  VENDOR’S  failure  to return  or  destroy  Confidential  Information  per  Provision  4,  above  may  result  in the UNIVERSITY or the U.S. Department of Education denying VENDOR access to Confidential Information for a period of at least five years.6.

 Immediate  Notice.

8391a371-d1a8-4bce-bf13-6ed39857a4b3.label: text_chunk
8391a371-d1a8-4bce-bf13-6ed39857a4b3.text: VENDOR  shall  give  UNIVERSITY  immediate notice of any unauthorized  use or disclosure of the Confidential Information, or of  any  breach  or  threatened  breach  by  VENDOR,  or  its  employees,  agents, contractors,  or  subcontractors  of  their  obligations  under  this  Agreement,  upon learning of same.7. Appropriate  Forum.

37ba1be0-33bb-4edc-bd44-95848d36e52d.label: text_chunk
37ba1be0-33bb-4edc-bd44-95848d36e52d.text:
VENDOR  and  UNIVERSITY  agree  that  this Agreement  is  entered  into  in  the  State  of  Arkansas  and  that  the  legal  forums located  in  the  State  of  Arkansas  are  the  appropriate  forums  in  the  event  any
2
party  seeks  legal  action  or  injunctive  relief  under  this  Agreement.

c5ef7fee-02b5-4996-87b0-c5be31b61aeb.label: text_chunk
c5ef7fee-02b5-4996-87b0-c5be31b61aeb.text: Both  parties consent to venue and personal jurisdiction in the appropriate forum in the State of Arkansas.8. Termination.    This  Agreement  shall  survive  the  termination  of  the services to be provided by VENDOR or any other agreement by and between the parties.9. Severability.  All of the provisions of this agreement are severable.

42b9f18c-6b89-4eae-8655-7f8d16475730.label: text_chunk
42b9f18c-6b89-4eae-8655-7f8d16475730.text: If any provision of this Agreement is rendered invalid or unenforceable by State or Federal statute or regulations or declared null and void by any court of competent jurisdiction,  the  remaining  provisions  of  this  Agreement  will  remain  in  full  force and effect.10.  Entire Agreement Amendments.

53677f8f-140a-433d-924d-883da10f597a.label: text_chunk
53677f8f-140a-433d-924d-883da10f597a.text: This Agreement is intended as the complete, final and exclusive statement of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes any prior agreements or understandings between the parties hereto.  This Agreement may be amended, modified or supplemented only by a written agreement signed by VENDOR and UNIVERSITY.

de1d1b6f-01f9-45d2-9861-548b9f910422.label: text_chunk
de1d1b6f-01f9-45d2-9861-548b9f910422.text: Any waiver of  the  terms  and  conditions  of  this  Agreement  must  be  in  writing  signed  by  the party granting such waiver and shall not operate as a waiver of, or estoppel with respect to, any subsequent or other failure to perform.11.  Original Copy.

45db6a61-4346-4a6b-b59e-43663f7389fa.label: text_chunk
45db6a61-4346-4a6b-b59e-43663f7389fa.text: Signed originals of this agreement will be kept on file in  the  Office  of  the  Information  Technology,  Arkansas  State  University, Jonesboro, Arkansas.  Copy of signed originals will be provided to VENDOR.IN  WITNESS  WHEREOF,  and  intending  to  be  legally  bound,  the  parties have executed this Agreement by their duly authorized representatives.

community_summary.0:
**Summary of Relationships Involving VENDOR and UNIVERSITY**

1. **Contractual Agreement with Arkansas State University-Jonesboro**: The VENDOR is entering into a contractual agreement with Arkansas State University-Jonesboro to provide goods or services, as evidenced by the presence of signature lines and titles in the documentation.

2. **Service Provision**: The VENDOR acknowledges a service relationship with the UNIVERSITY, indicating that it provides various services to the institution.

3. **Access to Confidential Information**: The VENDOR will have access to confidential student and employee information from the UNIVERSITY, establishing a significant relationship concerning information access and privacy.

4. **Contractual Relationship**: The VENDOR is a party to an agreement that outlines the terms and conditions under which services are provided, highlighting the formal contractual relationship between the VENDOR and the UNIVERSITY.

5. **Employment of Staff**: The VENDOR employs individuals responsible for delivering services to the UNIVERSITY, indicating a direct relationship between the organization and its workforce.

6. **Consultation on Security Breaches**: The VENDOR is required to consult with the UNIVERSITY regarding notifications to affected individuals in the event of a security breach, reflecting a collaborative approach to managing privacy and security issues.

7. **Notification of Legal Matters**: The VENDOR must notify the UNIVERSITY of any legal processes that may impact confidential information, indicating a formal obligation to keep the UNIVERSITY informed of legal matters.

8. **Disclosure of Confidential Information**: The VENDOR is required to give notice to the UNIVERSITY before disclosing any confidential information in response to legal processes, emphasizing the importance of communication regarding sensitive data.

9. **Possession of Confidential Information**: The VENDOR is responsible for holding and controlling confidential information as part of its operational obligations under confidentiality agreements.

10. **Authorization for Disclosure**: The VENDOR must obtain authorization from the UNIVERSITY to disclose confidential information to third parties, indicating that the UNIVERSITY regulates the VENDOR's actions concerning sensitive data.

11. **Legal Jurisdiction**: The VENDOR operates under the legal jurisdiction of the State of Arkansas, as specified in the agreement, which serves as the appropriate forum for any legal actions arising from the relationship.

Overall, these relationships illustrate a structured and formal interaction between the VENDOR and the UNIVERSITY, characterized by contractual obligations, service provision, confidentiality, and legal compliance.
community_summary.1:
The relationships outlined involve the concept of an "Agreement" and its interaction with "Judicial Review" and "Court" entities. 

1. **Agreement and Court**: The Agreement can be subjected to Judicial Review, where a Court has the authority to declare it null and void. This highlights the Court's significant role in overseeing the validity of the Agreement's provisions.

2. **Agreement and Party**: The Agreement is fundamentally a document that binds the involved party or parties to its terms and conditions, emphasizing the legal obligations and responsibilities established through the Agreement.

In summary, the Agreement is a critical legal document that involves parties bound by its terms, while the Court holds the power to review and potentially invalidate the Agreement through Judicial Review, underscoring the checks and balances within legal frameworks.
community_summary.2: The relationship between the entities "Parties" and "Confidential Information" is characterized by their involvement in the handling and agreement concerning this sensitive information. This relationship is central to their interactions, emphasizing the importance of confidentiality in their dealings. The parties are committed to managing and protecting the confidential information, which underlines the significance of trust and responsibility in their relationship.
community_summary.3: The Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act are both significant federal laws that address the privacy of sensitive information. The Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act focuses on the privacy of financial transactions, while the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act pertains to the privacy of educational records. Despite their different contexts, both laws share a common goal of protecting sensitive information.
community_summary.4:
The relationships between the UNIVERSITY, VENDOR, and the U.S. Department of Education highlight a structured framework of data management, oversight, and authority regarding Confidential Information.

1. **Disclosure Relationship**: The UNIVERSITY is willing to share Confidential Information with the VENDOR under specific conditions, indicating a trust-based relationship where sensitive data is exchanged.

2. **Management Responsibility**: The UNIVERSITY is tasked with the management and protection of Confidential Information, ensuring compliance with privacy laws and requiring express written consent for any disclosures. This underscores the UNIVERSITY's role as a steward of sensitive data.

3. **Protective Authority**: The UNIVERSITY possesses the authority to challenge legal processes faced by the VENDOR, reflecting its protective stance over the Confidential Information and its commitment to safeguarding it from potential misuse.

4. **Return of Materials**: The UNIVERSITY has the right to request the return of Confidential Information from the VENDOR, establishing a clear obligation for the VENDOR to comply, which reinforces the authority of the UNIVERSITY in this relationship.

5. **Regulatory Oversight**: The UNIVERSITY is under the oversight of the U.S. Department of Education, which can restrict the VENDOR's access to Confidential Information if the UNIVERSITY does not adhere to regulatory standards. This relationship emphasizes the importance of compliance and accountability in the management of sensitive data.

Overall, these relationships illustrate a comprehensive framework where the UNIVERSITY actively manages and protects Confidential Information while navigating regulatory requirements and maintaining authority over its interactions with the VENDOR.
community_summary.5: The relationships between the entities highlight the interconnectedness of State and Federal authorities in the legislative process. Both the State and Governmental Authority possess the power to enact statutes and regulations that influence the enforceability of provisions within agreements. This underscores the dual authority of both levels of government in shaping legal frameworks and ensuring compliance with established laws.
community_summary.6:
The summary of relationships involving Arkansas State University is as follows:

1. **Arkansas State University and the Office of the Information Technology**: The Office of the Information Technology is a department within Arkansas State University responsible for managing IT resources.

2. **Arkansas State University and VENDOR**: Arkansas State University has entered into a formal agreement with VENDOR, establishing a relationship for the provision of goods or services.

These relationships highlight Arkansas State University's organizational structure and its collaborative engagements with external vendors for operational support.
community_summary.7: The relationship between the "Agreement" and the "Providing Party" is characterized by the governing terms outlined in the Agreement. It specifies that the Agreement sets the conditions under which the Providing Party is entitled to seek remedies for any loss or damage resulting from a breach. This highlights the Agreement's role in defining the legal framework and protections available to the Providing Party in case of non-compliance.
community_summary.8:
The relationships outlined involve the "Receiving Party" and the "Providing Party," focusing on the management and handling of confidential information. 

1. **Receiving Party and Representatives**: The Receiving Party is permitted to share confidential information with its Representatives, who are authorized to access and manage that information, emphasizing the importance of trust and authorization in information sharing.

2. **Possession and Use of Information**: The Receiving Party holds responsibility for the possession and use of the Information, which must be restricted to a need-to-know basis among its Representatives. This highlights the necessity of limiting access to sensitive information to maintain confidentiality.

3. **Notification Obligations**: The Receiving Party is obligated to notify the Providing Party of any legal requests for disclosure of information. This establishes a communication channel between the two parties, ensuring transparency and compliance with legal requirements.

4. **Handling of Information**: The Receiving Party is tasked with handling the Information in accordance with legal standards and confidentiality obligations, indicating a duty to protect the integrity and confidentiality of the shared data.

5. **Retention of Information**: The Receiving Party has the right to retain certain portions of the Information for legal, regulatory, or internal compliance purposes. This indicates a formal relationship where the Receiving Party is empowered to keep specific information necessary for compliance.

Overall, these relationships underscore the critical responsibilities of the Receiving Party in managing confidential information, ensuring legal compliance, and maintaining effective communication with the Providing Party.
community_summary.9: The relationship between "Subject to" and "Information" emphasizes that the information exchanged between the parties is bound by confidentiality. This confidentiality mandates that the information must remain private and safeguarded against unauthorized access or disclosure. The reiteration of this relationship underscores the critical importance of protecting sensitive information in any interaction between the involved parties.
community_summary.10: The relationship between "Effective Date" and "Project" is significant as the Effective Date marks the commencement of the Project. It is defined as the date when the agreement is finalized, allowing the project to officially begin. This relationship underscores the importance of the Effective Date in initiating project activities.

community_summary.13:
The relationships summarized involve the entity "Confidential Information" and its connections to "Project" and "Trade Secret." 

1. **Confidential Information and Project**: Confidential Information is specifically designated for use in a particular Project, emphasizing that it can only be utilized by the Recipient for this defined purpose. This establishes a direct and exclusive relationship between the information and the Project's objectives.

2. **Relevance of Confidential Information**: The Confidential Information is not only used for the Project but is also shared within the context of the Project, highlighting its importance and relevance to the activities and goals associated with the Project.
